prevalence.source: WHO Europe / European psychiatric epidemiology syntheses
prevalence.trend: stable; diagnosis and maintenance care improving

burden.mortalityRate: Indirect; elevated suicide and comorbidity risk

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: DSM-5/ICD-11 criteria; structured assessment in specialist care; emphasis on distinguishing bipolar vs unipolar depression

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate to high with maintenance therapy and psychosocial support; varies by country

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Access to psychiatric assessment and monitoring is generally high in many member states
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Mood stabilizers (lithium, valproate, lamotrigine) and atypical antipsychotics; structured relapse prevention and psychoeducation
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Therapy access variability and comorbid substance use management
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Robust specialist care and monitoring capacity, with established maintenance pathways
